title: Statutory interpretation
summary: 9084 — literal, golden, mischief rules; purposive approach; aids to interpretation.
learningObjectives: Explain literal, golden, mischief, and purposive rules.
Use presumptions and rules of language.
Evaluate with cases: Fisher v Bell, Smith v Hughes, R v Allen.

simpleExplanation.title: What did Parliament mean?
simpleExplanation.summary: Judges use rules and aids to interpret ambiguous statutes — from strict literal meaning to filling gaps the mischief rule identifies.
steps: Literal — plain words, even absurd results.
Golden — modify literal to avoid absurdity.
Mischief — Heydon's Case, fix the gap.

sections.content: **Statutory interpretation** (1.1.4) — apply **rules + cases** to problem scenarios.
